info.title: Gigs Core API
info.description: OpenAPI spec for Gigs Core API endpoints

post.description:
Creates a new Connect Session for the given parameters.

Related guide: [Connect Sessions](https://developers.gigs.com/docs/connect/connect-sessions/about)

#### Preview
This endpoint is currently in preview and might change in the future.

We're excited to hear your feedback and ideas. Please send an email to support@gigs.com to share your thoughts.

201.description: Returns the newly created Connect Session.

get.description:
Retrieves the details of an existing Connect Session.

#### Preview
This endpoint is currently in preview and might change in the future.

We're excited to hear your feedback and ideas. Please send an email to support@gigs.com to share your thoughts.

200.description: Returns the Connect Session if it exists.

patch.description:
Updates the Connect Session with the given parameters.

The `user` or `userDetails` attributes can only be set when the session is not associated with a user yet.

Related guide: [Connect Sessions](https://developers.gigs.com/docs/connect/connect-sessions/about)

#### Preview
This endpoint is currently in preview and might change in the future.

We're excited to hear your feedback and ideas. Please send an email to support@gigs.com to share your thoughts.

200.description: Returns the add-on object if it exists.

patch.description: Updates an add-on's name and description.

post.description: Creates a new device with the given parameters.

200.description: Returns the device object if it exists.

delete.description: Retrieves the details of an existing device and deletes it.

patch.description: Updates the given properties of an existing device.

200.description: Returns a list of device model brands.
Returns a list of device models.
Returns a list of device models.
Returns the device model object if it exists.

post.description: Looks up network availability information based on geographic coordinates (latitude/longitude) or address. Returns network availability data only for US locations.

200.description: Returns the network availability data with location.
Returns a list of plan objects.
Returns the plan if it exists.

patch.description: Updates the specified plan by setting the values of the parameters passed.

200.description: Returns a list of document objects.
Returns the plan document if it exists.

get.description: Returns a list of portings. The portings returned are sorted by creation date, with the most recently created portings appearing first.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status to filter the portings by.

parameters.name: subscription
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the subscription to be filtered by.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to be filtered by.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination. The after parameter takes an object ID that defines the position in the list, only items immediately following the item with that ID will be returned.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination. The before parameter takes an object ID that defines the position in the list, only items immediately preceding the item with that ID will be returned.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The limit of items to be returned in the list, between 0 and 200.

200.description: Returns a dictionary with an items property that contains an array of portings.

post.description: Creates a new porting. It will be created in draft status and can be later used to create an order or subscription for the same provider.
Searches for existing portings matching the given parameters.

200.description: Returns a list of portings matching the search criteria.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing porting.

delete.description: Deletes a porting that is in the draft state.

patch.description: Updates a porting with the given parameters. Depending on the status of the porting, some fields may not be updated anymore.

post.description: Cancels a porting. A porting can be cancelled if it is in the pending, declined or informationRequired state, or in the requested state and scheduled for a future date.

get.description: Returns a list of service providers. It is recommended that callers provide either a recipient provider or country to filter for relevant service providers.

parameters.name: recipientProvider
parameters.description: Only return service providers that have the given network provider as supported porting recipient.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Only return service providers for the given country represented by the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.

200.description: Returns a dictionary with an items property that contains an array of service providers.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a service provider.

200.description: Returns the service provider object if it exists.

parameters.name: subscription
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the subscription to be filtered by.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of statuses to filter the port outs by.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The limit of items to be returned in the list.

200.description: Returns a dictionary with an items property that contains an array of port outs.

post.summary: Port a phone number into a subscription

200.description: Returns a list of subscription add-ons.

201.description: Returns the newly created subscription add-on.

200.description: Returns the subscription add-on if it exists.
Returns a list of subscription changes.

201.description: Returns the newly created subscription change.

200.description: Returns the subscription change if it exists.
Returns the subscription change after a successful deletion.

get.description: Lists usage records in ascending order for a subscription, defaulting to `daily` aggregation for the latest subscription period. If none of the `start`, `end`, or `period` parameters is provided, records are returned for the latest subscription period.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the project.

parameters.name: subscription
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the subscription.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Limits the usage data returned to the subscription period provided. This option is incompatible with the `start` and `end` parameters.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Limits the usage data to dates greater than or equal to the provided date. Can only be used in combination with `end`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Limits the usage data to dates up to and including the provided date. Can only be used in combination with `start`.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description: Determines the aggregation method used, defaulting to `daily`. `period` provides aggregated values for the time range or period requested. The `roaming` aggregation is in preview and may only be used in particular cases.

200.description: Returns the list of usage records.

post.description:
Simulates usage on a subscription with a test SIM. Can be used to test usage notifications. Subscription must be in status "active".

#### Preview
This endpoint is currently in preview and might change in the future.
We’re excited to hear your feedback and ideas. Please send an email to support@gigs.com to share your thoughts.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the project.

parameters.name: subscription
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the subscription.

get.description:
Returns a list of usage balances for the given project. The list can be filtered by subscription, subscription period, or subscription add-on. When filtering by subscription without specifying subscriptionPeriod or subscriptionAddon, all usage balances for the subscription are returned regardless of source type or period.

#### Preview
This endpoint is currently in preview and might change in the future.
We’re excited to hear your feedback and ideas. Please send an email to support@gigs.com to share your thoughts.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the project.

parameters.name: subscription
parameters.description: Filter usage balances by Subscription ID.

parameters.name: subscriptionPeriod
parameters.description: Filter usage balances by subscription period. Requires the `subscription` parameter. Accepts a positive integer, the special value `current`, or a negative integer to request previous periods (e.g., `-1` for the previous period). Cannot be combined with `subscriptionAddon`. A usage balance is sourced from either a subscription period or a subscription add-on, never both - combining these will return an error.

parameters.name: subscriptionAddon
parameters.description: Filter usage balances by Subscription Add-on ID. Can be combined with `subscription` but not with `subscriptionPeriod`. A usage balance is sourced from either a subscription period or a subscription add-on, never both — combining these will return an error.

200.description: Returns a list of usage balances.

get.description:
Returns the details of an existing usage balance.

#### Preview
This endpoint is currently in preview and might change in the future.
We’re excited to hear your feedback and ideas. Please send an email to support@gigs.com to share your thoughts.

parameters.name: user

200.description: Returns the address after a successful deletion.

get.description: Returns a list of addresses owned by the given user. The addresses returned are sorted by creation date, with the most recently created addresses appearing first.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the project.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the user.

200.description: Returns a list of address objects.

post.description:
Create a new address for the given user with the provided parameters.

If any validations fail during the user address creation process, detailed information will be provided in the error response body. If applicable, suggested alternative values for properties that do not pass the validations will also be included.

Please note that there is a maximum limit of 10 active addresses permitted per user. Attempts to exceed this limit will result in an error response. Addresses must also be unique for a given user.

data.description: Amount of data used in bytes.

roaming.description: The roaming mode the usage occurred in.

subscription.description: The unique identifier for the subscription to which the usage is attributed.

sms.description: Amount of SMS messages sent and received.

smsInternationalMessages.description: Amount of SMS messages sent internationally.

smsLocalMessages.description: Amount of SMS messages sent locally.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the aggregation was last updated.

voice.description: Amount of voice usage in seconds.

voiceInternationalSeconds.description: Amount of international voice usage in seconds.

voiceLocalSeconds.description: Amount of local voice usage in seconds.

data.description: Amount of data used in bytes.

dataDevice.description: Amount of on-device data used in bytes.

dataTethering.description: Amount of tethering data used in bytes.

voice.description: Amount of voice usage in seconds.

voiceLocal.description: Amount of local voice usage in seconds.

voiceInternational.description: Amount of international long distance voice usage in seconds.

sms.description: Amount of SMS messages sent and received.

smsLocal.description: Amount of local SMS messages sent and received.

smsInternational.description: Amount of international long distance SMS messages sent and received.

subscriptionAddon.description: The unique identifier for the subscription addon to which the simulated usage should be attributed.

country.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code to assign to the simulated usage.

roaming.description: The roaming mode to assign to the simulated usage.

usageBalance.description: The Usage Balance id to assign the simulated usage to.

usageBalanceAmount.description: The amount of usage to attribute to the specified Usage Balance.

type.description: The entity type providing the allowance.

subscriptionPeriod.description: When the allowance is provided by the subscription's plan, the subscription period this usage balance is bound by.

subscriptionAddon.description: When the allowance is provided by a Subscription Add-on, the subscription add-on this usage balance is provided by.

id.description: Unique identifier for the usage balance.

subscription.description: Unique identifier of the Subscription the usage balance is related to.

unit.description: The unit the usage is counted in.

used.description: The amount of usage consumed, counted in `unit`.

limit.description: The amount of usage permitted by the associated allowance counted in `unit`. A value of `null` indicates an unlimited allowance.

remaining.description: The amount remaining that can still be consumed, counted in `unit`. A value of `null` indicates an unlimited allowance.

usedPercent.description: The percentage of `limit` that has been used, expressed as an integer between 0 and 100. A value of `null` indicates an unlimited allowance.

remainingPercent.description: The percentage of `limit` that has not been used, expressed as an integer between 0 and 100. A value of `null` indicates an unlimited allowance.

usableFrom.description: Timestamp representing the beginning of this usage balance's validity.

usableUntil.description: Timestamp representing the end of this usage balance's validity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user.

createdAt.description: Time when the user was created.

email.description: The primary email address of the user. Must be unique across all users.

emailVerified.description: Whether the user's primary email address is verified or not.

fullName.description: The user's full name. Required for some Plans.

preferredLocale.description: The user's locale preference represented as an IETF language tag.

status.description: The current status of the user.

email.description: The primary verified email address of the user.
The primary email address of the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the address.

country.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the address.

createdAt.description: The time the address was created.

line1.description: The first line of the address, e.g. street and house number.

line2.description: The second line of the address, e.g. apartment number.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address.

user.description: Unique identifier for the address user.

country.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the address.

line1.description: The first line of the address, e.g. street and house number.

line2.description: The second line of the address, e.g. apartment number.

state.description: The state/province/region of the address. Required for US/CA addresses to be a valid ISO 3166-2 2 letter code.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address. Required for countries with postal codes.

PaymentMethod.description: Payment methods are used to process payments for subscriptions and orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the payment method.

brand.description: Card brand. Only available for type `card`.

createdAt.description: Time when the payment method was created.

last4.description: The last four digits of the card. Only available for type `card`.

provider.description: The provider of the payment method. Currently only `stripe` is supported.

status.description:
The status of the payment method. It can have one of the following values:

    - `available`: The payment method is available for processing payments.
    - `requiresConfirmation`: The payment method requires user confirmation before being able to process payments.

user.description: Unique identifier for the user of the payment method.

Payment.description: Payments describe the amount charged for an order or subscription using a given payment method.

id.description: Unique identifier for the payment.

amount.description: The total amount charged for this payment.
The price amount in the currency's minor unit, e.g. "cents" for many currencies.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code. Must be a supported currency.

attemptedAt.description: Time when the payment was last attempted.

createdAt.description: Time when the payment was created.

disputeClosedAt.description: Time when the payment dispute was closed. Only present if the payment is disputed.

disputeOutcome.description: The outcome of a payment dispute. Only present if the payment is disputed.

failedAttempts.description: The number of failed payment attempts so far.

failureCode.description: An error code indicating the reason for the payment failure. Only present if the payment failed.

failureMessage.description: A human-readable message indicating the reason for the payment failure. Only present if the payment failed.

reason.description:
The reason for this payment. It can have one of the following values:

    - `subscriptionCreation`: The payment is due to a new subscription.
    - `subscriptionRenewal`: The payment is due to a recurring subscription renewal.
    - `other`: The invoice is not related to a subscription but due an e.g. one-time addon.

references.description: List of payment references. Supported reference types are `order` and `subscription`.

object.description: The type of the referenced object.

id.description: Unique identifier of the referenced object.

refunded.description: The total amount refunded from this payment.

amount.description: The price amount in the currency's minor unit, e.g. "cents" for many currencies.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code. Must be a supported currency.

refundedAt.description: Time when the payment was fully refunded. Only present if the payment was refunded.

status.description:
The status of the payment. It can have one of the following values:

    - `pending`: The payment has been created but not yet processed by the payment provider.
    - `succeeded`: The payment was completed successfully.
    - `failed`: The payment failed. Payments are retried automatically for up to 3 weeks.
    - `refunded`: The payment was fully refunded. Partially refunded payments do not have this status.
    - `disputed`: The payment is under dispute.
    - `requiresConfirmation`: The payment requires user confirmation before being processed.
    - `requiresPaymentMethod`: The payment is awaiting for a payment method to be attached.

user.description: Users are end consumers of subscriptions. Users can have multiple devices, SIMs, and subscriptions.

theme.description: The theme setting that will influence the visual appearance during the Connect Session.

Intent.description: The intent of the connect session.

IntentCreate.description: The intent of the connect session to create.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Connect Session.

callbackUrl.description: The URL to which the user will be redirected after the Connect Session is completed.

url.description: The session URL. Redirect your users to this URL to take them to Connect. It is only present on all create responses or in the update responses that associate the session with a user. It should not be stored, logged or exposed to anyone other than the user.

user.description: The unique identifier of the user that the session is for.

callbackUrl.description: The URL to which the user will be redirected after the Connect Session is completed.

user.description: The unique identifier of the user.

callbackUrl.description: The URL to which the user will be redirected after the Connect Session is completed.

user.description: The unique identifier of the user.

amount.description: The price amount in the currency's minor unit, e.g. 'cents' for many currencies.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code. Must be a supported currency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the credit note.

createdAt.description: Time when the credit note was created.

creditTo.description: Specifies where the credit amount should be credited to.

fileUrl.description: A signed URL to download the credit note PDF file.

invoice.description: The unique identifier for the invoice that the credit note applies to.

lineItems.description: The line items for the credit note.

status.description: The status of the credit note.

subtotal.description: The total amount credited before any taxes or fees.

tax.description: The sum of the amounts in taxes credited for each line item.

total.description: The total amount credited after taxes and fees.

voidedAt.description: Time when the credit note was voided.

id.description: Unique identifier for the invoice.

address.description: The unique identifier for the address this invoice relates to.

appliedBalance.description: The amount of user balance applied to pay this invoice.

createdAt.description: Time when the invoice was created.

discount.description: The total discount applied. This is the sum of the discounts of each line item.

dueAt.description: Time when the invoice is due to be paid.

fileUrl.description: A signed URL to download the invoice PDF file.

finalizedAt.description: Time when the invoice was finalized and could no longer be changed.

lineItems.description: The line items that make up the invoice.

overdueAt.description: Time when the invoice is considered overdue.

paidAt.description: Time when the invoice was paid.

payment.description: The unique identifier for the payment associated with the invoice, if any.

period.description: The subscription period that this invoice relates to.

id.description: Unique identifier for the quote.
Unique identifier for the voucher.

createdAt.description: Time when the voucher was created.

amount.description: The discount amount in the currency's minor unit.

priceDiscount.description: The price amount of the discount applied via the voucher.

priceDiscountPercentage.description: The percentage amount of the discount applied via the voucher.

type.description: Whether and how the voucher discount recurs.

durationInMonths.description: For how many months is the discount applied via the voucher.

recurrence.description: Details on whether and for how long the discounts applied via the voucher recur.

redemptions.description: Number of times the voucher has been redeemed.

retiredReason.description: Label indicating the reason why the voucher is retired.

Metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Individual keys can be unset by posting an empty value to them.

Actor.description: Represents the entity that performed an action.

id.description: Unique identifier for the actor.

type.description:
The type of the actor. It can have one of the following values:

    - `apiKey`: An API key was used to perform the action.
    - `connectSession`: A Connect Session was used to perform the action.
    - `system`: The action was performed by the system.
    - `user`: The action was performed by a user.

APIKey.description: API keys are used to authenticate requests to the API.

id.description: Unique identifier for the API key.

name.description: An optional name for the API key.

project.description: Unique identifier for the project that the API key belongs to.

createdAt.description: Time when the API key was created.

Coverage.description: Represents the coverage details for a plan, including supported countries and networks.

countries.description: List of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where the plan provides coverage.

country.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the network.

networks.description: List of networks included in the coverage.

DeprecatedPortOutCredentials.description: Deprecated port out credentials. Use PortOutCredentials instead.

id.description: Unique identifier for the device model brand.

name.description: The display name of the device model brand.

Period.description: Represents a time period with a start and end date.

start.description: The start date and time of the period.

end.description: The end date and time of the period.

PropertyErrorDetail.description: Provides additional details about a property validation error.

message.description: A human-readable error message describing the validation error.

path.description: The path to the property that caused the validation error.

VirtualNumber.description: A virtual number assigned to a subscription.

id.description: Unique identifier for the virtual number.

createdAt.description: Time when the virtual number was created.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number in E.164 format.

status.description: The status of the virtual number.

Forbidden.description: The authenticated user does not have permission to perform this request.

NotFound.description: The requested resource does not exist.

UnprocessableEntity.description: The request was well-formed but could not be processed due to a semantic error.

NotImplemented.description: The requested operation is not implemented.
